parks.name: Channel Islands
parks.detail: Channel Islands National Park encompasses five remarkable islands and their ocean environment, preserving and protecting a wealth...

postImages.title: Inspiration Point by Tim Hauf
postImages.caption: Inspiration Point, Anacapa Island: One of the most spectacular views in the park can be found from Inspiration Point. Looking to the west, one may see Middle and West Anacapa, with Santa Cruz Island in the distance.

postImages.title: Arch Point by Tim Hauf
postImages.caption: Arch Point, Santa Barbara Island: With winter rains, the coreopsis emerges from summer's dormancy with light green foliage and bright yellow daisy-like flowers

postImages.title: Torrey Pines by Tim Hauf
postImages.caption: Torrey Pines, Santa Rosa Island: Torrey pines occur naturally in only two locations throughout the world--on the cool, fog drenched northeastern slopes of Bechers Bay and just north of San Diego.

postImages.title: Point Bennet by Tim Hauf
postImages.caption: Point Bennet, San Miguel Island: One of the greatest concentrations of wildlife in the world occurs on San Miguel with over 100,000 pinnipeds gathering to breed, pup, and rest.

postImages.title: Island Fox by Tim Hauf
postImages.caption: Island Fox, Santa Cruz Island: Thousands of years of isolation in a unique island environment has resulted in the development of the endemic island fox, a dwarf form of the mainland gray fox.

postImages.title: Climbing Mount Rainier

postImages.title: Wildflower Meadow Along the Wonderland Trail

postImages.caption: Deep in Zion's Wilderness site Kolob Arch, one of the largest free-standing arches in the world.
